Inside the app
Field test day
This isn't stock photography. First morning ScoutIQ ran in the field, it called the drainage and two herds showed. The tool earns its spot in your pack.

A note from Jake and Jon

We built this because we're hunters who were tired of guessing. We tested it in the field last weekend and it put us on two herds. But it's early. It won't always be right, and some things are still rough around the edges.

Hunting is what we love, and that's the whole reason this exists. We'll keep making it better on the regular. We both have day jobs, so if a fix takes a few days, that's why. Be patient with us, and tell us what the map gets right and wrong. That's how this gets good for all of us.

Jake & Jon · ScoutIQ
FAQ
What is ScoutIQ?

ScoutIQ is a predictive scouting app for Colorado elk hunters. Instead of showing static maps, it forecasts daily elk movement by combining terrain, habitat, weather, hunting pressure, and lunar data, so you know where and when elk are most likely to move.

Is ScoutIQ free?

Yes. ScoutIQ is free during its Colorado public beta. You create an account to get access to the scouting tool.

What states and species does it cover?

The beta covers elk across Colorado units on public land. More states and species are planned as the model improves.

How is it different from onX or GOHUNT?

Mapping apps like onX and GOHUNT show you the land, unit boundaries, and public access. ScoutIQ adds a prediction layer on top, forecasting where elk are likely to be and when they are likely to move based on current conditions.

What data does it use to predict elk movement?

ScoutIQ analyzes terrain and habitat, real-time weather, barometric pressure, lunar cycles, thermals, and hunting pressure, then weights the forecast by confidence so predictions stay grounded.

This is an early build. If the forecast is off for your unit, tell us. Accuracy reports are the most valuable thing you can send.
